topic: Is sync realistic as primary income? The honest answer after 90 days
hook: Month 1 answer: "Sync can be significant secondary income."

slides.eyebrow: Education · Is sync realistic as primary income? The honest answer after 90 days
slides.headline:
Month 1 answer: "Sync can be significant secondary income."
Month 3 answer — after 3 confirmed placements and 90 days of data.
slides.lede: ↓ · Here's what you need to know: — — — · WHAT THE NUMBERS SHOW

slides.headline: Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede: A single confirmed placement generates: · Upfront sync fee: $800–$3,000 for regional / short-term (typical entry range) · Master fee (if you control your master): adds 30–50% to the upfront total · Performance royalties: ongoing for 1–3 years per placement, through your PRO
list_items: Upfront sync fee: $800–$3,000 for regional / short-term (typical entry range)
Master fee (if you control your master): adds 30–50% to the upfront total
Performance royalties: ongoing for 1–3 years per placement, through your PRO

slides.headline: The work, in numbers.

stats.label: million streams = roughly $3,000–$4,000 at standard rates

slides.lede: Here's what you need to know: Sync wins on timeline — if you can get the placements. · — — — · WHAT MAKES IT VIABLE AS PRIMARY INCOME

slides.headline: Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede: Critical variables: · Catalogue size: 30–60 sync-ready tracks (not 300 rough tracks) · Placement rate: one per quarter is sustainable; one per month is scalable · PRO registration: every placement must be registered to collect performance income · Here's what you need to know: — — — · THE HONEST LIMITS
list_items: Catalogue size: 30–60 sync-ready tracks (not 300 rough tracks)
Placement rate: one per quarter is sustainable; one per month is scalable
PRO registration: every placement must be registered to collect performance income
Repeat brief flow: supervisors who return = predictable pipeline

slides.headline: Sync is not passive income.
slides.lede: Each placement requires documentation, brief response, negotiation, and delivery. · It's also not consistent month-to-month. A quiet month with no briefs is followed by a month with four.

slides.headline:
What sync offers that streaming doesn't:
— Significantly higher per-use
slides.lede:
income
— Performance royalty compounding over years
— Industry relationships that generate further work

slides.headline:
What streaming offers that sync doesn't:
— Volume (thousands of micro-transactions vs.
slides.lede:
dozens of large ones)
— Speed (release today, earn this month)
— No gatekeeping (no vetting required) · Here's what you need to know: — — — · THE HONEST ANSWER · Yes — with 3+ confirmed placements per year, PRO registration, and a documented catalogue of 40+ tracks, sync can be primary income.

slides.headline: No — if you haven't completed rights documentation, don't have stems, or
slides.lede: haven't registered with a PRO. · The talent is there. The infrastructure has to catch up first.

slides.headline: Open briefs. Every Tuesday.
slides.lede: Composers: apply once, get matched for life. Supervisors: shortlist in 48h.
